issue_title: Documentation doesn't have versions set properly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

When doing the 21.1 release the documentation didn't have the version set properly and it wasn't marked as the latest release.

This has been fixed manually, but not to be fixed in the release process for 21.1 and main.

### Version

21.1

### Expected behavior

Versions set correctly

### Actual behavior

Versions are blank and the docs are not set as the latest

### How to Reproduce?

Run the release process

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix setting versions for docs (#19826)

Closes #19801

issue_title: Export / import tests do not clean environment
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

Export / import tests set a system property (export action) which is not cleaned after the export or import test has finished. This leads to hard to investigate situations where a following test misbehaves and attempts to perform the export/import based on previous test's flow.

### Version

21

### Expected behavior

Test clean up the environment after run

### Actual behavior

System property remains set.

### How to Reproduce?

Run tests and check the property

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix export / import tests relict

Closes: #19812

issue_title: Testsuite must rely on IDs from Keycloak
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

The testsuite often creates a new object and assumes its ID. However Keycloak may introduce a different ID for the object than requested originally e.g. because of the ID format does not conform the underlying store.

Update the testsuite to determine IDs rather than expect it created as expected.

### Version

21

### Expected behavior

IDs are determined after creation from Keycloak

### Actual behavior

IDs are suggested in the REST call and then assumed their existence

### How to Reproduce?

Run any of the affected tests with a file store (`map-storage-file` testsuite profile), e.g. `AdminClientTest`

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not impose assumptions on ID format

Closes: #19814

issue_title: Some IDs not encoded properly in authz client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

The authz-client currently relies on the assumption that the resource IDs are UUIDs. However Keycloak may introduce a different ID for the object than requested originally e.g. because of the UUID format does not conform the underlying store.

Update the respective URL creation code to encode the ID in URL correctly.

### Version

21

### Expected behavior

IDs are correctly encoded

### Actual behavior

IDs are concatenated as-is.

### How to Reproduce?

Run any of the authz tests with a file store (`map-storage-file` testsuite profile)

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Properly encode id in URL

Closes: #19816

issue_title: Support for realm-less entities in login failures
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16676 introduced support for realm-less entities but omitted login failures. This issue is to fix this state.

### Version

21

### Expected behavior

Login failure provider sets the realm for entities into the storage context even when realm is not explicitly set in the entity.

### Actual behavior

Login failures do not infer the entities from the provider.

### How to Reproduce?

Attempt to use login failures in the context of file store.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Support for realm-less entities in providers (login failures)

Closes: #19818

issue_title: Keycloak issues on edge and after chrome upgarde to 112 (with experimental features)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

In [login status iframe](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/resources/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/login-status-iframe.html) document.hasStorageAccess is called before checking the cookie.
This results in two issues:
1.  postMessage doesn't use the correct origin causing the message to get lost
2.  it prevents checking the cookie, and notifying parent document... even though getCookie() works. 
@jonkoops sonce you are the one who implemented this 6 months ago, I understand what you tried to do: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/14466 but I think you are using the wrong check. hasStorageAccess retrning false doesn't seem to correlate with cookies access.

### Version

21.1.0

### Expected behavior

login-status-iframe iframe should work with granting storage access

### Actual behavior

it does not and causes other errors because it doesn't fail gracefully. (origin missing in postMessage).

### How to Reproduce?

run keycloak with login-status-iframe in edge

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message: Improve third-party storage access detection and cookie fallback

issue_title: Upgrade from 21.0.2 to 21.1.0 fails on oracle db
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When updating keycloak 21.0.2 to 21.1.0 the database gets changed. 
jpa-changelog-21.1.0.xml tries to change the dataType of some columns.
This fails with the messages:
```
{​​​​​​​​"timestamp":"2023-04-20T10:56:56.122Z","sequence":8465,"loggerClassName":"org.jboss.logging.Logger","loggerName":"org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler","level":"ERROR","message":"ERROR: Migration failed for changeset META-INF/jpa-changelog-21.1.0.xml::21.1.0-19404::keycloak:\n Reason: liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: ORA-01439: column to be modified must be empty to change datatype\n [Failed SQL: (1439) ALTER TABLE KEYCLOAK_MASTER_TEST.RESOURCE_SERVER_POLICY MODIFY DECISION_STRATEGY NUMBER(3)]","threadName":"main","threadId":1,"mdc":{​​​​​​​​}​​​​​​​​,"ndc":"","hostName":"keycloak-77d7b6c65c-ptd8z","processName":"QuarkusEntryPoint","processId":211}​​​​​​​​
{​​​​​​​​"timestamp":"2023-04-20T10:56:56.122Z","sequence":8466,"loggerClassName":"org.jboss.logging.Logger","loggerName":"org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler","level":"ERROR","message":"ERROR: ORA-01439: column to be modified must be empty to change datatype\n [Failed SQL: (1439) ALTER TABLE KEYCLOAK_MASTER_TEST.RESOURCE_SERVER_POLICY MODIFY DECISION_STRATEGY NUMBER(3)]","threadName":"main","threadId":1,"mdc":{​​​​​​​​}​​​​​​​​,"ndc":"","hostName":"keycloak-77d7b6c65c-ptd8z","processName":"QuarkusEntryPoint","processId":211}​​​​​​​​
{​​​​​​​​"timestamp":"2023-04-20T10:56:56.123Z","sequence":8467,"loggerClassName":"org.jboss.logging.Logger","loggerName":"org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler","level":"ERROR","message":"ERROR: ORA-01439: column to be modified must be empty to change datatype\n","threadName":"main","threadId":1,"mdc":{​​​​​​​​}​​​​​​​​,"ndc":"","hostName":"keycloak-77d7b6c65c-ptd8z","processName":"QuarkusEntryPoint","processId":211}​​​​​​​​
``` 

### Version

21.1.0

### Expected behavior

Update of the column types.

### Actual behavior

Server crash.

### How to Reproduce?

Setup Keycloak 21.0.2 from docker image sourced at quay.io against an oracle database.
Configure it, that the table RESOURCE_SERVER_POLICY gets an entry.
Run Keycloak 21.1.0 against the same database.
View it failing to start.

### Anything else?

In issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19404 the broken changelog was introduced.
commit_message:
Rewrite changelog SQL to make it work on Oracle

Closes #19841

issue_title: NPE when updating a subflow in an authentication flow
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

It is not possible to update an subflow in the authentication flow when the description field is null in the database (NPE): 

`2023-04-20T11:33:13.601+0000 ERROR - org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler : Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "String.equals(Object)" because the return value of "org.keycloak.models.AuthenticationFlowModel.getDescription()" is null
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.AuthenticationManagementResource.updateExecutions(AuthenticationManagementResource.java:675)`

### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

Updating the name or description of a subflow, where the description is null in the database, does not lead to an NPE.

### Actual behavior

Update the name or description of a subflow, where the description is null in the database, does lead to an NPE and thus the subflow i not longer editable.

### How to Reproduce?

**Prerequests**: Create a subflow in an authentication flow and set the description of the subflow in the database to null.
Then follow the steps below: 

1. Log in to the admin UI and select an authentication flow
2. Edit the name or the description of the subflow
3. An error occurs instead of updating the subflow

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
NPE when updating a subflow in an authentication flow

closes #19844

issue_title: Keycloak Quarkus Server dependency broken
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dependencies

### Describe the bug

The keycloak quarkus server dependency has keycloak account ui/keycloak admin ui as a dependency of it. Because these two don't exist in the latest version 21.0.0 I get errors resolving keycloak. This also bricks the [keycloak custom server](https://github.com/SamTV12345/keycloak-custom-server) installation which is based on [this](https://github.com/thomasdarimont/keycloak-custom-server) example on how to run keycloak in an alpine container. 

### Version

21.1.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak account ui/admin ui can be resolved from Maven

### Actual behavior

Keycloak account ui/admin ui are not resolvable as they are not present in the version 21.1.0

### How to Reproduce?

Check out the project and main. Try resolving dependencies

### Anything else?

I would love to have these two dependencies still available so I don't have to build them from scratch. They were present up until Keycloak migrated to monorepo
commit_message:
Include account-ui and admin-ui in deployment to Maven repo (#19860)

Closes #19850

issue_title: Admin UI does not respect default values for custom authenticator configurations
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I have a [custom extension](https://github.com/sventorben/keycloak-home-idp-discovery) that implements an authenticator. The authenticator is configurable and defines default values for config options. As it can be seen in the screenshot, the default values are correctly send from the backend to the frontend. However, the frontend seems to ignore them and does not show the default values in the configuratoin dialog.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12183470/233485623-e54f9b41-9a46-417e-bd88-de01697b53f4.png)


### Version

20.0.5, 21.1.0

### Expected behavior

New configuration uses default values for all config options.

### Actual behavior

Default values are not used.

### How to Reproduce?

- Checkout the extension at https://github.com/sventorben/keycloak-home-idp-discovery
- build it with `mvn clean install`
- start a docker container with docker-compose: ``docker-compose up`
- navigate to https://localhost:8080 and login to Keycloak with user admin and password admin
- open the realm named `test-realm`
- open the flow named `home-idp-discovery-flow`
- click the gear icon for the `Home IdP Discovery` authenticator

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
added default value back when not new (#20235)

fixes: #19852

issue_title: Not possible to override default or built-in providers
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

In previous versions of Keycloak it was possible to override a built-in provider of an SPI by simply deploying a custom provider with the same provider ID.

This was not strictly supported, but quite a few users where relying on this.

This was broken in 21.0.0 by this commit:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/a804400c842d9e1198a1c24b520498b1466c877f#diff-eea4abb789496423cf703ca63d968147abef72d694dae64b59b06153e3ca379d

In addition it should be possible to override the default provider by either providing a new provider for the SPI with a higher order:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/6016ebd442862bb0bc088d17f243d096ab2fd76f/server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/provider/ProviderFactory.java#L58

Alternatively by explicitly setting a different default provider for the SPI. Neither of these are well documented, and not sure how the default provider for an SPI is configured in the Quarkus distribution.


### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

It should be possible to:

* Set the default provider for an SPI through CLI/env/properties
* Override the default provider for an SPI by deploying a provider with higher order
* Explicitly override a built-in provider with a specific ID. This should be an explicit thing rather than implicit like how it used to work.

### Actual behavior

It is not well documented how to set a different default provider for an SPI either through order, or server config.

It is not possible to override a built-in provider by ID since 21.0.0.

### How to Reproduce?

Try to deploy a replacement for a default provider, such as EmailProvider.

Try to override a built-in authenticator by ID.

### Anything else?

Related issues:

* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19805
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19530
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19335
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19777
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19596
commit_message:
Ability to override default/built-in providers with same providerId. Using ProviderFactory.order() for choosing priority providers
Closes #19867

issue_title: Usability issues with composite role management
issue_body:
### Description

The Admin Console (version 21.1) provides the following view for a newly created client role:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/789496/233618635-0158dcfd-72ac-484c-a280-cd0fa76f3d2d.png)

## Adding associated roles hard to find

Issue: Adding associated roles is hidden at the action menu. It would be better to have the "Associated Roles" tab always present (also when no role was added). Should look like is when you add and remove an associated role (except the "Composite" flag):
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/789496/233613038-44427174-5f08-49f9-ad86-9e24ec664220.png)

Issue: The text from realm roles is reused here, but the context is a client role.

## Searching for client roles a bit cumbersome

When clicking "Assign role", realm roles are always pre-selected.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/789496/233615870-cb16e5e1-753d-4b60-8665-6d073c85415c.png)

Issue: Before you can search for a client role you need to switch to "Filter by client roles". It would be more convenient to search at client and realm roles at same time, without enforcing a selection by type.

## Removing all roles at unexpected place
Once one or more roles are assigned, all of the can be removed within the action button.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/789496/233613974-cb795ec0-e9dc-4a45-9921-c493d6897a2b.png)

Issue: Removing all associated roles shouldn't be a top level action. It is expected at level of the tab "Associated Roles"

## Selecting all roles adds the roles from this page plus one
When all roles of a page are selected, this number plus one is assigned. 
How to reproduce: Search for "master-realm" client roles and then select all (with default page size 10)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/789496/233621352-649e40ba-270a-49ed-a652-2e33bf153188.png)
After assignment 11 roles are assigned:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/789496/233621909-eb0c0d72-d311-46af-aa9e-6f97e47637c5.png)

Expected: All roles are assigned (independently of page size) or just the ten visible roles are assigned. 

## Selecting a role and then searching again keeps it selected
When selecting one role and then narrowing down the search, the first selected role is still selected even if not visible any more. It will be assigned to to together with the selected roles which are visible.

Issue: this could be unexpected, as the selection state isn't visible any more.




### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Make it easier to understand and use Keycloak role configuration. 

### Details

_No response_
commit_message:
Enhanced associated roles (#30968)

Signed-off-by: Agnieszka Gancarczyk <agancarc@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Agnieszka Gancarczyk <agancarc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Saving client admin-cli in master realm gives a javascript error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I start the latest Keycloak with docker:

`docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest start-dev`

Then I visit the admin portal and go to `Clients`. I select the `admin-cli` client, of the master realm. On the Settings tab, I scroll down and change `Client authentication` to "On", uncheck the box `Direct access grants`, and check the box `Service accounts roles`.

I finally click the `Save` button.

Nothing happens. 

When I press F12 to see the Dev tools console log, I can see an error:

```
Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: u is undefined
    validate LogoutPanel.tsx:139
    validateField index.esm.mjs:804
    wt index.esm.mjs:1609
LogoutPanel.tsx:139:22
```

### Version

21.1.0

### Expected behavior

To see a "Saved successfully" confirmation. Or an error message telling me why I couldn't save.

### Actual behavior

Nothing happens when I click Save, and an error is displayed on the javascript console log.

### How to Reproduce?

Steps to reproduce are in the description of the bug.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
added check for undefined (#19949)

fixes: #19883

issue_title: Admin UI hangs with many subgroups
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

This was reported internally by Engin Atick from Claudia's team.  He has groups with thousands of subgroups.  When he tries to open the group section, the UI hangs and then errors out.

I have reproduced this using partial import of a group with 10,000 subgroups.  The old UI seems to handle it better.  With the old UI, the response is slow but it does work.

Here is a json you can import to create the groups:
[groups-10000-in-subgroup.zip](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/11326066/groups-10000-in-subgroup.zip)


### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Groups display

### Actual behavior

Freeze and crash

### How to Reproduce?

Use attached json to do partial import.  Then open groups section.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Keycloak 21.1.1: Paging and filtering not working in "Assign roles" popup for Groups
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the new Admin UI, in "Role Mapping" tab for a group, when clicking on "Assign roles" button, the popup will display only the first page of role results. Clicking on next, will display the same first page of results, so its not possible to display subsequent pages.

When entering search term in "Search by role name" field, the search term doesn't affect the result, but the same unfiltered first page of results is displayed.

The requests sent to keycloak server:

/groups/b06c2caa-7742-4747-9f96-8fcd78c43cc8/role-mappings/realm/available?first=0&max=11 - result contains all 21 roles (I have 21 in the realm), but the UI displays only first 10
/groups/b06c2caa-7742-4747-9f96-8fcd78c43cc8/role-mappings/realm/available?first=10&max=11 - result contains all 21 roles (I have 21 in the realm), but the UI displays only first 10
/groups/b06c2caa-7742-4747-9f96-8fcd78c43cc8/role-mappings/realm/available?first=0&max=11&search=test - result contains all 21 roles (I have 21 in the realm), but the UI displays only first 10

Theories: it's either a backend issue, or the parameter names sent from the frontend are incorrect.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

* Clicking on next should display next page instead of first page only
* Entering search term should display only matching roles


### Actual behavior

* Clicking on next shows first page only
* Entering search term has no effect on search results (non-matching roles are retrieved)


### How to Reproduce?

* Add more then 10 roles to the realm
* See that paging and filtering on "Assign roles" popup don't work


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
on the roles page we have paging on others not (#20102)

fixes:  #19981

issue_title: Default Roles show all roles if "Hide inherited roles" is not checked
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the default roles section of the Keycloak Administration Console all roles are shown if the "Hide inherited roles" checkbox is not checked. I don't believe this is intended, it lets the user believe that alle roles are assigned to new users even if that is not the case.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

If the "Hide inherited roles" checkbox is not checked in the default roles section, all assigned default roles should be shown as well as all the roles that are inherited from the default roles. Inherited roles can be associated roles if the default role is a composite role.

### Actual behavior

If the "Hide inherited roles" checkbox is not checked in the default roles section, all roles are shown.

### How to Reproduce?

- Go to Realm settings -> User registration -> Default roles
- Deselect the "Hide inherited roles" checkbox

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Only show default role effective roles (#20285)

fixes: #19982

issue_title: JavaScript Authenticator Providers not updated automatically on build and SQL error when removing/adding in flow
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

If you package a Javascript provider and deploy it to a jar, add the scripts to a flow, and then edit and redeploy the script, re build the server and start, the scripts is still in the old versions

Workaround : Remove the scripts from the flow and add it again is the only fix I found.

Edit : Actually this workaround worked the first time but now it's even worst

If I remove the Script from the flow and if I add it again I got the error

```
2023-04-27 20:11:36,510 WARN  [org.mariadb.jdbc.message.server.ErrorPacket] (executor-thread-4) Error: 1062-23000: Duplicate entry 'script-role-based-access.js' for key 'PRIMARY'
2023-04-27 20:11:36,511 WARN  [org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.spi.SqlExceptionHelper] (executor-thread-4) SQL Error: 1062, SQLState: 23000
2023-04-27 20:11:36,511 ERROR [org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.spi.SqlExceptionHelper] (executor-thread-4) (conn=542) Duplicate entry 'script-role-based-access.js' for key 'PRIMARY'
```

`script-role-based-access.js' is the name of my scripts here


### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Should update the script

### Actual behavior

Script already in the flow are not updated

### How to Reproduce?

- Install a Javascript providers (in my case an authenticator)
- Start the server and the script to a flow (in my case Browser flow copy)
- edit the content of the script, rebuild the jar `my-scripts.jar`
- update`providers/my-scripts.jar` and rebuild `bin/kc.sh build --features=scripts`then start

Now if you go in your flow and display the preview of your script, you still see the previous content

I tried to remove/rename the jar without success

Only fix for me was to remove the script from the flow and add it again (which is an easy workaround) but this doesn't always works second time I had

```
2023-04-27 20:11:36,510 WARN  [org.mariadb.jdbc.message.server.ErrorPacket] (executor-thread-4) Error: 1062-23000: Duplicate entry 'script-role-based-access.js' for key 'PRIMARY'
2023-04-27 20:11:36,511 WARN  [org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.spi.SqlExceptionHelper] (executor-thread-4) SQL Error: 1062, SQLState: 23000
2023-04-27 20:11:36,511 ERROR [org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.spi.SqlExceptionHelper] (executor-thread-4) (conn=542) Duplicate entry 'script-role-based-access.js' for key 'PRIMARY'
```

Fix was 

`DELETE FROM AUTHENTICATOR_CONFIG_ENTRY WHERE AUTHENTICATOR_ID='script-role-based-access.js';`
`DELETE FROM AUTHENTICATOR_CONFIG WHERE ID='script-role-based-access.js';`

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix authenticatorConfig for javascript providers
Closes #20005

issue_title: Conditional user attribute authenticator does not match the joined groups
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

The conditional user attribute authenticator does not match the groups joined by the given user.

### Version

21.1.0

### Expected behavior

Attributes of the joined groups should also be taken into account while matching the condition.

### Actual behavior

Only the user attributes are matched:

```
        boolean result = user.getAttributeStream(attributeName).anyMatch(attr -> Objects.equals(attr, attributeValue));
        return negateOutput != result;
```
[ConditionalUserAttributeValue.java](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f7988a6ae335836df910f0b5fbb6f1d4b3b80fee/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/conditional/ConditionalUserAttributeValue.java#L47)

### How to Reproduce?

Create an authentication flow with the given condition on user attribute `x` equals to `X`, and create two users:

- `u1` with  a user attribute `x` equals to `X`
- `u2` with no attributes but joined to a group `g2` having the same attribute `x` equals to `X`

Try to login: only `u1` is authenticated, while `u2` is rejected

### Anything else?

I think (but still have to validate) that the conditional user role authenticator is instead matching the group roles: if not I will raise a different issue
commit_message:
Extends the conditional user attribute authenticator to check the attributes of the joined groups (#20189)

Closes #20007

issue_title: authenticator javaScript Provider always failed the login, user context is lost and break the login
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I have a JavaScript provider, packaged in a jar and well usable on the keycloak instance

This provider use the variable `user` to get username and to check role with user.hasRole(role)

Unfortunately the user variable is always null. Even with a very basic script added to the default Browser flow. 

We was on keycloak 15 before and the script was exactly the same and working well, 
[bug-report-script.csv](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/11347350/bug-report-script.csv)

Other variable such as `context`, `client` works well

This is the output of my scripts we can see in logs that there is no probleme to use `client` but `user` is null

```
2023-04-27 21:34:56,173 INFO  [org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.browser.ScriptBasedAuthenticator] (executor-thread-7) user null
2023-04-27 21:34:56,197 INFO  [org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.browser.ScriptBasedAuthenticator] (executor-thread-7) Checking access to authentication for client 'My Website' through mandatory role 'feature:authenticate' for user 'anonymous'
2023-04-27 21:34:56,203 ERROR [org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.browser.ScriptBasedAuthenticator] (executor-thread-7) org.keycloak.scripting.ScriptExecutionException: Could not execute script 'Check Feature Authenticate' problem was: TypeError: null has no such function "hasRole" in <eval> at line number 44
2023-04-27 21:34:56,203 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-7) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: org.keycloak.authentication.AuthenticationFlowException
```

Also, in the the keycloak log I have

```
2023-04-28 16:54:38,525 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-9) type=LOGIN_ERROR, realmId=hbp, clientId=xwiki, userId=null, ipAddress=127.0.0.1, error=invalid_user_credentials, auth_method=openid-connect, auth_type=code, response_type=code, redirect_uri=https://wiki-dev.ebrains.eu/, code_id=dae8b423-f4cf-47aa-884f-84c2435dc579, response_mode=query
```

We can see that somehow, just switching the JavascripProvider from disabled to required and the user context is lost when going through the script

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

User variable should not be null in a javaScript Provider used in the authentication browser flow

### Actual behavior

is always null

### How to Reproduce?

Edit :You can use the docker image and instruction provided in the first answer of this issue

Or ...

- Build a jar with an authenticator 'check-role.js' JavaScript following javaScript Provider requirement https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_development/index.html#_script_providers

You can use the official demo one and just check the `user` variable null
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/resources/scripts/authenticator-template.js

Or you can use mine in attachement, replace `csv`by `js`. Basically my scripts just check presence of a specific role for a user using `user.hasRole(role)` so this role need to be added in the client and assign to the user

- Duplicate the authenticator Browser flow
- Add Step at the very end with our script
- bind the flow to browser
- try to login using the test client in real test, this will fail in null pointer exception when the script use the `user` variable


### Anything else?

if there is no workaround, it is a critical/blocking issue for our company and a no go for the upgrade unfortunately.
commit_message:
More docs clarification for script authenticator (#20444)

* More docs clarification for script authenticator
closes #20009


Co-authored-by: andymunro <48995441+andymunro@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: Group attribute query is missing QueryParams in java admin client
issue_body:
### Description

Using the Java admin client library it is currently not possible to query attributes with additional parameters. Thus, the parameter described in the REST API  (first, max, and briefRepresentation) cannot be set when using the admin-client library.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

The admin client library should match the REST API documentation.

### Details

#12964 implemented the group attribute search. However the GroupsResource is misssing the additional method with all QueryParams. For reference: [the UsersResource ](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9186327807477f4ca5c36d0adb6fd7b7f62b9721/integration/admin-client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/admin/client/resource/UsersResource.java#L120) implements all parameters.
commit_message:
Extend admin-client GroupsResource: Support the query functionality to be used in combination with the parameters first, max and briefRepresentation

Closes #20016

issue_title: Processing of env variable references in config file broken
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

it seems that the env variables handling in keycloak.conf is broken in 21.1.1 - env variable references are not resolved anymore.

The following entry in keycloak.conf worked fine with 20.0.5:
````
   log-level=INFO,com.foo.bar:${FOO_LOG_LEVEL:info}
````
but fails in 21.1.1 with an error:
````
2023-04-28 14:42:47,958 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Failed to start server in (development) mode
2023-04-28 14:42:47,958 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Invalid log category format: com.foo.bar:"${FOO_LOG_LEVEL:INFO}". The format is 'category:level' such as 'org.keycloak:debug'. The format is 'category:level' such as 'org.keycloak:debug'.
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.Messages.invalidLogCategoryFormat(Messages.java:65)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.configuration.mappers.LoggingPropertyMappers.resolveLogLevel(LoggingPropertyMappers.java:178)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.configuration.mappers.PropertyMapper.transformValue(PropertyMapper.java:208)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.configuration.mappers.PropertyMapper.getConfigValue(PropertyMapper.java:139)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.configuration.mappers.PropertyMappers.getValue(PropertyMappers.java:47)
	at org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.configuration.PropertyMappingInterceptor.getValue(PropertyMappingInterceptor.java:51)
````

I think the problem was introduced by this commit: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/9874da150e5003cf1b19bd2ab95f097cf3ffe4f0


### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Env Variable references should be correctly resolved or use the provided default.

### Actual behavior

Server fails to start with env variable references in keycloak.conf file.

### How to Reproduce?

Add a config entry like `log-level=INFO,com.foo.bar:${FOO_LOG_LEVEL:info}` to keycloak.conf and try to start the server.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix processing of env variable references (#20462) (#20493)

Closes #20032

(cherry picked from commit 1aa3e2d7e341ba167d3f509cc3de04223cef90f8)

issue_title: Use an original domain name of Kerberos Principal in UserModel attribute instead of configured value of Kerberos realm in User federation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

There are 2 Active Directory domains (EXAMPLE1.COM and EXAMPLE2.COM) which have mutual trust.
Configuring User Federation of kerberos against one of them (EXAMPLE**1**.COM), RH-SSO/Keycloak can authenticate user1@EXAMPLE**2**.COM against the domains.
However, the "KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL" attribute in UserModel is "user1@EXAMPLE**1**.COM". It should be "user1@EXAMPLE**2**.COM".


### Version

21.0.2/upstream

### Expected behavior

It should be "user1@EXAMPLE**2**.COM".

### Actual behavior

"user1@EXAMPLE**1**.COM".

### How to Reproduce?

1. Setup AD domains of EXAMPLE1.COM and EXAMPLE2.COM then make mutual trust between them
2. configure Keycloak User federation so that it authenticates users agaist EXAMPLE1.COM
3. log in Keycloak as user1@EXAMPLE2.COM

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Use an original domain name of Kerberos Principal in UserModel attribute instead of configured value of Kerberos realm in User federation
closes #20045

issue_title: Custom theme - url.resourcesCommonPath references wrong theme
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

In a custom theme with own `template.ftl` it is not possible to reference resources (in this case CSS) in the parent theme it is using. The theme in question references "keycloak in theme.properties:

```
parent=keycloak
```

Since the update from 20.0.2 to 21.1.1 the references inside the ftl are forced to use the active theme

| 20.0.2 | 21.1.1 |
| ------ | ------ |
| ![20 0 2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6563013/235657432-3d528d6f-cdfd-4262-b4b5-6d2e5fa4384d.png) | ![21 1 1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6563013/235657449-dde72423-d5d3-4768-912c-7df6fcee3506.png) |

See how the patternfly css files are correctly retrieved in the "keycloak" theme in 20.0.2, but are retrieved from "nx-swl" in 21.1.1. In the latter they don't exist, as they are simply inherited from the "keycloak" theme.

The `template.ftl` gets those stylesheets using the following code
```
<link rel="icon" href="${url.resourcesPath}/img/favicon.ico" />
<#if properties.stylesCommon?has_content>
	<#list properties.stylesCommon?split(' ') as style>
		<link href="${url.resourcesCommonPath}/${style}" rel="stylesheet" />
	</#list>
</#if>
```


### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Retrieve those stylesheets the way 20.0.2 did (referencing the theme where they are exist)

### Actual behavior

Force files to be included in the custom theme, rather than using those from the theme they are provided.

### How to Reproduce?

Perform steps from https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_development/#_themes
The theme used to reproduce this locally is a login theme only if this matters.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Duplicated serverPrincipal property in LDAPStorageProviderFactory
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Rendering a custom user-federation in the admin-ui fails, because of duplicated children ids "serverPrincipal":
`Encountered two children with the same key, `serverPrincipal`. `

Looking at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.LDAPStorageProviderFactory the property for "serverPrinicipal" is added 2 times:

1. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPStorageProviderFactory.java#L208
2. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPStorageProviderFactory.java#L225

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Property is only added once, so the page is rendered correctly.

### Actual behavior

There are missing labels and the page is not usable to create a new custom user-federation.

### How to Reproduce?

Add a new LDAPStoreageProvider and try to create an instance via admin-ui.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Removing duplicated serverPrincipal at LDAPStorageProviderFactory.java

closes #20101

commit_message:
Improve quoting and variable parsing for batch start script. (#20212)

Quote some specific variables used in the batch script to address blank
space issues when Keycloak is in a directory structure containing
spaces.

Address some variable parsing issues by using delayed expansion instead
of default behavior.

Remove superfluous SetLocal EnableDelayedExpansion

Closes keycloak/keycloak#20115

issue_title: Support for non-XA databases
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Looks like we are no longer supporting the `RESOURCE_LOCAL` transaction type in the default PU but forcing JTA. See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/17664/commits/05417c48b82d5cd2b85b8803578e624c12260efc.

Not sure about the motivation behind the change above but some databases do not work well if not disabling JTA completely.

We also have tests to cover those use cases. For that, see `org.keycloak.it.storage.database.MSSQLTest`. Although this test is out-dated and not run in our CI, we need to make sure the changes herein linked are not going to introduce backward compatibility issues.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

Make it possible to use databases that does not support XA.

### Actual behavior

Failing to run using a database that does not support XA.

### How to Reproduce?

Run the `org.keycloak.it.storage.database.MSSQLTest`.

### Anything else?

Possible related issues:

* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16192
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12379
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12061
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/10339
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/10191
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/9133
commit_message: Support for non-XA databases (#20141)

issue_title: GitHub actions log "ApiKey is not exists!" when trying to upload test results
issue_body:
### Description

All test runs report on pull requests the following error: 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3957921/235934508-6c5ebd5a-6599-4f90-b297-4c696d903008.png)

This is due to the API Key which is missing on pull requests for security reasons. So the action shouldn't run when that key is not available.

```
Prepare all required actions
Getting action download info
Download action repository 'runforesight/foresight-test-kit-action@v1.3.1' (SHA:dbbd1f185f5dbce7[2](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/4859087324/jobs/8661271517?pr=20004#step:7:2)[3](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/4859087324/jobs/8661271517?pr=20004#step:7:3)6c7b38137[4](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/4859087324/jobs/8661271517?pr=20004#step:7:4)e3dbcc2a6972)
Run ./.github/actions/upload-surefire-reports
Run runforesight/foresight-test-kit-action@v1.3.1
[Foresight Test Kit] Start getting job info...
Error: [Foresight Test Kit] Request failed with status code 404
Error: [Foresight Test Kit] ApiKey is not exists! Data can not be send.
```

Example: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/4859087324
commit_message:
Remove Foresight from GitHub workflows (#20146)

Closes #20122

issue_title: Role mapping tab no longer visible when using fine grained permissions after upgrade from 20.0.3 to 21.0.2
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

We have been using fine grained permissions with Keycloak [20.0.3](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/20.0.3).

A user using fine grained permissions was able to view the `Role mapping` tab when navigating a `Group` found in `Groups` in the admin UI.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/171731/235934840-3c20aada-dfa4-4988-b42a-85c370eb6195.png)

The issue: this tab "disappeared" after upgrading Keycloak to version [21.0.2](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/21.0.2).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/171731/235935150-c816e64f-bc51-457f-951c-80d57f55282c.png)

Note:
* No Keycloak configuration or permissions changes had been made meanwhile
* We also tested to see whether upgrading to version [21.1.1](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/21.1.1) would resolve it, which it unfortunately did not


### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

A user using fine grained permissions should be able to view the `Role mapping` tab when navigating a `Group` found in `Groups` in the admin UI.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/171731/235934840-3c20aada-dfa4-4988-b42a-85c370eb6195.png)


### Actual behavior

A user using fine grained permissions is unable to view the `Role mapping` tab when navigating a `Group` found in `Groups` in the admin UI.

This tab "disappeared" after upgrading Keycloak to version [21.0.2](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/21.0.2).


### How to Reproduce?

- in a Keycloak instance version `20.0.3`
- setup basic fine grained permissions for a specific user with a groups "manage" role linked to a policy granting that specific user these permissions
- verify that the user is able to see for a group in groups, the `Role mapping` tab
- upgrade the Keycloak instance to version `21.0.2`

expected:
- the user is able to see for a group in groups, the `Role mapping` tab 

actual:
- the user does not longer see the `Role mapping` tab


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Make role mapping tab visible with view user permissions (#26386)

Co-authored-by: Salem Wafi <32916450+SalemWafi@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Sebastian Schuster <sebastian.schuster@bosch.io>

issue_title: Searching for multiple types in the Events section gives an error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/client/node

### Describe the bug

The request that's being made is:
http://localhost:8180/admin/realms/master/events?first=0&max=11&type=SEND_RESET_PASSWORD%2CUPDATE_CONSENT_ERROR

it should be 
http://localhost:8180/admin/realms/master/events?first=0&max=11&type=SEND_RESET_PASSWORD&type=CUPDATE_CONSENT_ERROR

the error is that arrays are being "stringified"
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8cb202eb290bd258049e3ddbe0c5ec50169c3192/js/libs/keycloak-admin-client/src/utils/stringifyQueryParams.ts#L20

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

the right request being made, this is the only place where arrays are used

### Actual behavior

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/51133/236005325-45baa547-b12e-4bcf-b8aa-82ffea62c593.png)


### How to Reproduce?

go to the events section and search for 2 event types

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Properly handle array query arguments in Admin Client (#24483)

Closes #20135
